summary: The Goshen Township Board of Trustees held their regular meeting featuring a significant police recognition ceremony honoring officers for drug interdiction, an armed robbery investigation, a shooting apprehension, tornado response leadership, and Officer of the Year. The board also discussed zoning code amendments with new consultant Liz Fields from McBride-Dale-Clarion, approved various motions including police extra duty pay increases and road vacation, and hired a part-time service worker.
decisions: Motion to adopt the agenda – passed
Motion to approve payment of bills (Feb 28 – Mar 13, 2023) – passed
Motion to approve Police Policy 504 revisions raising extra duty detail pay to $50/hr for schools/mobile home parks and $65/hr for construction/other – passed
Motion to vacate public roadway stub on Mustang Circle for Drees Homes development – passed
Motion to sponsor a Salute to Leaders table at a cost of $700 – passed
Motion to approve ongoing payment of CDL renewals ($26.50) for service department employees – passed
Motion to buy back one grave from Barbara Rowland at Meyers Cemetery Section B, Lot 38, Grave 5 at $500 – passed
Motion to set special meeting zoning workshop for March 21 at 6:30 PM – passed
Motion to adjourn to executive session under ORC 121.22 G1 – passed
Motion to hire Chris Hilton as part-time service worker at $15.50/hr with one-year probationary period and CDL requirement – passed
Motion to adjourn at 8:19 PM – passed
action_items: Administrator Rose to put VVA veteran banner information on the township website
Trustee Hauserman to attend Farm Bureau solar energy briefing on March 15
Administrator Rose to send survey for second zoning workshop date (around April 10-12)
Liz Fields to research agricultural definitions and exemptions for zoning workshop
Liz Fields to prepare options regarding accessory structures on lots without principal structures for workshop
Liz Fields to research pre-filling BZA variance resolutions to eliminate 30-day wait time
Liz Fields and Bob Rose to bring previously tabled lot size/square footage zoning changes back for discussion
Administrator Rose to notify BZA and Zoning Board members about March 21 workshop
Director Meadows to complete pre-employment screening for Chris Hilton
key_amounts: $40 to $50/hr increase for extra duty detail pay for school/mobile home park functions
$50 to $65/hr increase for extra duty detail pay for construction and other details
$700 for Salute to Leaders table sponsorship
$26.50 per CDL renewal reimbursement for service department employees (every four years)
$500 buyback of one grave at Meyers Cemetery
$15.50/hr pay rate for new part-time service worker Chris Hilton
$80,000 AFG grant application submitted by fire department
$1.7 million SAFER grant application submitted for staffing
$1.2 million per home in new Lucky Homes development on Oakland Road (26 homes)
notable_topics: Police recognition ceremony – Drug Interdiction Awards, Dollar General armed robbery investigation, State Route 28 shooting apprehension, tornado response leadership, Officer of the Year (Tyler Smith)
Station 18 fire station funding – working through critical infrastructure code requirements with legal team; no levy or bond issue planned at this time

Zoning code amendments discussion with new consultant Liz Fields (McBride-Dale-Clarion) – accessory structures on adjacent lots, accessory structures in front yards, panhandle/flag lot requirements, administrative relief for area standards
Solar farm development concerns across Clermont County – Farm Bureau briefing scheduled
Lucky Homes 26-home development on Oakland Road – potential 2024 Homearama site
Vacation of Mustang Circle stub road for Drees Homes expansion of Villages of Belmont
Service garage repairs by Teasdale Fenton nearing completion
BZA variance resolution process improvement to eliminate delays
Scheduling of zoning workshop for March 21 with trustees, BZA, and Zoning Board members
attendees: Fire Chief (unnamed, gave invocation and Station 18 update)
Officer Tyler Smith (Officer of the Year 2022)
notes: The meeting had a significant ceremonial component with the police recognition ceremony taking up a large portion of the meeting, followed by a 15-minute recess with refreshments prepared by the Citizens Police Academy Alumni Association (CPAA). The board went into executive session under ORC 121.22 G1 regarding employment matters. The Fire Chief mentioned being recognized nationally in Firehouse Magazine for the Park Avenue Fire response. There was notable discussion about the township's image concerns with the Loveland mailing address being assigned to developments in Goshen Township. The zoning discussion was preliminary with no decisions made, serving as preparation for the March 21 workshop.
